mnemonics: P.P.P. (Page Faults Paralyze Performance) - Thrashing occurs when Processes generate too many Page Faults, causing the system to spend all its time swapping Pages instead of Performing useful work.
keyPoints: Thrashing is a state where the system spends more time swapping pages in and out of memory than executing processes.
It is caused when the degree of multiprogramming is too high, and the processes do not have enough frames allocated to hold their working sets.
It can be resolved by reducing the degree of multiprogramming (suspending processes) or by increasing the amount of available physical memory.
commonMistakes: Confusing thrashing with general system slowdown; thrashing specifically refers to excessive paging activity.
Believing that adding more processes always improves system utilization, when it can actually lead to thrashing and a drastic decrease in CPU utilization.
